events.event_description: U.S. Legation in Brussels established when Chargé d'Affaires Hugh Legare presented his credentials.

evidence.quote: The U.S. Legation in Brussels was established on September 25, 1832, when Chargé d'Affaires, Hugh Legare, presented his credentials in Brussels.

events.event_description: U.S. Legation in Brussels elevated to an Embassy.

evidence.quote: The U.S. Legation in Brussels was elevated to an embassy on October 3, 1919.

events.event_description: U.S. Embassy in Brussels closed following the German invasion of Belgium.

evidence.quote: The United States closed its embassy in Brussels on July 15, 1940, after the German invasion of Belgium.

events.event_description: Embassy near the Belgian Government-in-exile in England re-established, with Rudolph E. Schoenfeld designated as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

evidence.quote: Rudolph E. Schoenfeld was designated on November 1, 1940, to serve as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim near the Government of Belgium established in England.

events.event_description: U.S. Embassy in Brussels reestablished with Ernest de W. Mayer as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

evidence.quote: The U.S. Embassy in Brussels was reestablished on September 14, 1944, with Ernest de W. Mayer as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.
